name: Hot-Smoked Trout
recipeIngredient: Hardwood smoking chips (preferably alder or fruitwood)
recipeInstructions:
1. Filet the fish, leaving the skin on. If a completely boneless fillet is desired, use tweezers or clean needle-nose pliers to remove the pin bones running down the middle of the fillet near the head end.
2. Place the fillets skin side down in a glass or stainless steel baking pan or other deep dish.
3. Combine the salt and sugar and sprinkle a generous layer over the fish, more thickly on the thickest part of the meat, and slightly less on the tail and edges. Use about one tablespoon total for each 2-pound fish, using the full amount for a 3-pounder.
4. Cover and refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight.
5. Drain off any collected juices in the pan. Add cold water to cover, let stand for 15 minutes, then drain and repeat. Drain well and pat dry with paper towels.
6. For a smoother surface, lay the fillets on a wire rack in a cool, breezy place for 15 minutes to dry.
7. Build a small charcoal fire (10 to 12 briquets) at one edge of a covered grill and let it burn down until covered with gray ash.
8. Meanwhile, soak half a cup of the smoking chips in water.
9. Cover the grill and adjust the vents on the top and bottom to maintain a temperature of about 200 degrees F. Drain the chips and add them to the coals; replace the grill with one handle nearest the fire to facilitate adding smoking chips.
10. Lay the fillets on the opposite side from the fire, with the thickest parts nearest the heat.
11. Cover and cook until the fish is opaque, 30 to 40 minutes. Add some dry smoking chips to the fire every 15 minutes or so to maintain smoke and heat.
12. Serve hot or cold.
PER SERVING: 285 calories, 36 g protein, 0 g carbohydrate, 15 g fat (3 g saturated), 105 mg cholesterol, approximately 240 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.
